channel.title: front-end on David's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in front-end on David's Blog

item.title: React Basics & Deployment
item.description: 1. Create project with Vite 1 2 3 4 npm create vite@latest name-of-your-project -- --template react cd <your new project directory> npm install react-router-dom localforage match-sorter sort-by npm run dev/npm run build The Proxy in vite.config.js just works for development mode. In production mode it doesn’t work. The production mode means you run npm run build which will generate a dist folder. Then you can deploy that folder into your server.

item.title: useEffect Hook in React
item.description: 1. When useEffect is called What does useEffect do? By using this Hook, you tell React that your component needs to do something after render. React will remember the function setup you passed, and call it later after performing the DOM updates. 1 useEffect(setup, dependencies?) No Dependency (No Array): If you don’t provide the second argument, the effect runs after every rendering. Empty Array ([]): If you pass an empty array, the effect runs once after the initial rendering.
